finding_summary: Three MKM cards in the agency.md cohort are misattributed as carrying the Detective creature subtype when Scryfall canonical type-lines confirm they do NOT. Verified via direct Scryfall API: Sanguine Savior MKM-230 = `Creature — Vampire Cleric` (no Detective); Furtive Courier MKM-59 = `Creature — Merfolk Advisor` (no Detective); Gadget Technician MKM-204 = `Creature — Goblin Artificer` (no Detective). The confab recurs across 3 nodes: (a) **agency.md** lines 64, 74, 80, 82, 125, 127 — multiple loci asserting these cards are Detectives; (b) **orzhov-signet.md** line 52 — 'winged Detective Vampire Cleric' for Sanguine Savior; (c) **230-sanguine-savior.md** card body line 107 — 'Disguise + Detective subtype wire to the R.A.M.I.'. The pattern: vision/trivia pass mistook Disguise-keyword carriers + MKM-cohort-membership for Detective-subtype-bearing, and three card type-lines were over-claimed. Of the 14 agency.md cohort cards verified via Scryfall, only 4 carry the Detective subtype: Agency Outfitter (Sphinx Detective), Mistway Spy (Merfolk Detective), Reckless Detective (Devil Detective), Undercover Crocodelf (Elf Crocodile Detective). The other 5 creature cards in the cohort use Disguise OR investigate-mechanics but their canonical creature-type subtypes are NOT Detective (Agency Coroner = Ogre Cleric, Crowd-Control Warden = Centaur Soldier, Furtive Courier = Merfolk Advisor, Gadget Technician = Goblin Artificer, Sanguine Savior = Vampire Cleric). The agency.md line 127 'Agency officers are unified by FUNCTION, not creature-type' Caveats already explicitly says the Agency is NOT defined by Detective subtype — yet line 82 and adjacent claims contradict this self-stated discipline by attaching the Detective subtype to cards that don't carry it. The same Caveats line says 'Vampire Cleric Sanguine Savior' (correct!) but adjacent bullet says 'Sanguine Savior as Orzhov-recruited Detective' (wrong). Self-contradiction within the same Caveats section. The Reckless Detective MKM-141 description as 'depicted goblinoid' at agency.md line 72 is a separate minor visual-misidentification (the card is a Devil per Scryfall, not a goblinoid) which the fix below also corrects.

fixes.before: - **Furtive Courier** (MKM-59, uncommon, Mark Behm art) — small pink-haired pointy-eared courier rushing down an arched stone stairwell clutching a folded envelope, glancing back over a shoulder with tense anxiety. Mechanically a `{1}{U}` Detective creature with sacrifice-an-artifact-unblockable + attack-trigger draw-then-discard. Flavor speaker: *Linghu of the Foundway Associates* — the Foundway Associates are an Agency-affiliated investigative network named across MKM flavor, locating the courier as Agency-side messenger labor rather than freelance underworld delivery. The card is the Agency's clandestine-delivery operational register: case-evidence in transit.
fixes.after: - **Furtive Courier** (MKM-59, uncommon, Mark Behm art) — small pink-haired pointy-eared courier rushing down an arched stone stairwell clutching a folded envelope, glancing back over a shoulder with tense anxiety. Mechanically a `{1}{U}` Merfolk Advisor (per Scryfall canonical type-line — NOT a Detective despite Agency-flavor attribution) with sacrifice-an-artifact-unblockable + attack-trigger draw-then-discard. Flavor speaker: *Linghu of the Foundway Associates* — the Foundway Associates are an Agency-affiliated investigative network named across MKM flavor, locating the courier as Agency-side messenger labor rather than freelance underworld delivery. The card is the Agency's clandestine-delivery operational register: case-evidence in transit. The Merfolk Advisor subtype (vs. Detective) reads as the Agency's clandestine-messenger auxiliary — a courier who serves Agency casework without being a field investigator themselves; mirrors the Agency Coroner (Ogre Cleric, not Detective) pattern on the Foundway-side messenger function.
fixes.rationale: Correct line-74 type-line attribution. Furtive Courier is canonically a Merfolk Advisor per Scryfall, not a Detective. Add the auxiliary-function framing already established for Agency Coroner (which the body correctly identifies as Ogre Cleric, not Detective).

fixes.before: - **Reckless Detective** (MKM-141, uncommon, Tuan Duong Chu art) — a small fanged creature with a tail, claws, and a wide-brimmed detective's hat charges forward through a burning interior, one arm raised holding a rounded object aloft, the other extended forward with claws spread. Mechanically a red attack-trigger creature: sacrifice an artifact or discard a card on attack, then draw a card and gain +2/+0. The card's name carries "Detective" — the canonical R.A.M.I. creature subtype introduced in *Murders at Karlov Manor* and used on 85+ MKM-and-reprint prints — and the depicted goblinoid charging a burning crime scene is the Agency's red-coded reckless-rogue operational archetype. Where Burden of Proof and Eliminate the Impossible render Agency procedure as deliberation, this one renders Agency procedure as chaos: an Agency detective whose investigative method is *charge in and improvise*.
fixes.after: - **Reckless Detective** (MKM-141, uncommon, Tuan Duong Chu art) — a small fanged creature with a tail, claws, and a wide-brimmed detective's hat charges forward through a burning interior, one arm raised holding a rounded object aloft, the other extended forward with claws spread. Mechanically a red Devil Detective (per Scryfall canonical type-line: `Creature — Devil Detective`) attack-trigger creature: sacrifice an artifact or discard a card on attack, then draw a card and gain +2/+0. The card's name carries "Detective" — the canonical R.A.M.I. creature subtype introduced in *Murders at Karlov Manor* and used on 85+ MKM-and-reprint prints — and the depicted devil-figure charging a burning crime scene is the Agency's red-coded reckless-rogue operational archetype. Where Burden of Proof and Eliminate the Impossible render Agency procedure as deliberation, this one renders Agency procedure as chaos: an Agency detective whose investigative method is *charge in and improvise*.
fixes.rationale: Correct line-72 visual misidentification. Reckless Detective is canonically a Devil per Scryfall (`Creature — Devil Detective`), not a 'goblinoid'. Adds the explicit Scryfall type-line citation for the Detective subtype claim (which IS canonical for this card, distinguishing it from the three actual misattribution cases).

fixes.before: - **Gadget Technician** (MKM-204, common, Caio Monteiro art) — gleeful goblin-type figure in goggles and leather apron, examining a skull-faced thopter drone with blue propeller blades inside a brass-and-teal workshop cluttered with vials, chains, and crystals. Mechanically a `{2}{U/R}` Detective Goblin Artificer with ETB-or-flip Thopter-token creation + Disguise. The card is the Agency's UR-coded forensic-engineering operational register: the technical-craft side of investigative procedure, with the thopter as the Agency's signature surveillance device (parallel to the forensic-thopter coding established on Agency Outfitter's tutored Magnifying Glass and Thinking Cap).
fixes.after: - **Gadget Technician** (MKM-204, common, Caio Monteiro art) — gleeful goblin-type figure in goggles and leather apron, examining a skull-faced thopter drone with blue propeller blades inside a brass-and-teal workshop cluttered with vials, chains, and crystals. Mechanically a `{2}{U/R}` Goblin Artificer (per Scryfall canonical type-line — NOT a Detective despite Agency-flavor attribution and Disguise mechanic) with ETB-or-flip Thopter-token creation + Disguise. The card is the Agency's UR-coded forensic-engineering operational register: the technical-craft side of investigative procedure, with the thopter as the Agency's signature surveillance device (parallel to the forensic-thopter coding established on Agency Outfitter's tutored Magnifying Glass and Thinking Cap). The Goblin Artificer subtype (vs. Detective) reads as the Agency's mechanical-specialist auxiliary — a tinker who serves Agency casework without being a field investigator themselves; mirrors the Agency Coroner (Ogre Cleric, not Detective) pattern on the artifact-engineering function.
fixes.rationale: Correct line-80 type-line attribution. Gadget Technician is canonically a Goblin Artificer per Scryfall, not a Detective. Adds the auxiliary-function framing parallel to Agency Coroner.

fixes.before: - **Sanguine Savior** (MKM-230, common, Valera Lutfullina art) — winged figure in dark armor carrying an unconscious companion through foggy gothic skies above a city skyline, with a small sun motif visible on a distant spire AND a larger embossed sun-medallion on the carrier's armor chest — the canonical [[orzhov-signet]] eclipsed-sun iconography rendered at jewelry scale on a Detective-cycle card. Mechanically a `{2}{W/B}` Flying / Lifelink Detective Vampire Cleric with Disguise + face-up creature-buff trigger. Dual-coded: Agency operational function (Disguise + Detective subtype wire to the R.A.M.I. operational register), Orzhov institutional identity (the canonical Syndicate eclipsed-sun signet visible on chest armor and architecture per the `orzhov-signet` symbol node). This is the structural case the Agency's institutional-autonomy thesis predicts: a Detective whose institutional secondary identity is an Orzhov-recruited operative — extraction-faction labor reassigned to investigation-faction work without losing the medallion. Symbol-layer cross-edge: `symbols: ["orzhov-signet"]`; character-layer cross-edge: `characters: ["agency", "orzhov-syndicate"]`.
fixes.after: - **Sanguine Savior** (MKM-230, common, Valera Lutfullina art) — winged figure in dark armor carrying an unconscious companion through foggy gothic skies above a city skyline, with a small sun motif visible on a distant spire AND a larger embossed sun-medallion on the carrier's armor chest — the canonical [[orzhov-signet]] eclipsed-sun iconography rendered at jewelry scale on an MKM Disguise-cohort card. Mechanically a `{2}{W/B}` Flying / Lifelink Vampire Cleric (per Scryfall canonical type-line — NOT a Detective despite MKM Disguise-cohort membership) with Disguise + face-up creature-buff trigger. Dual-coded: Agency operational function (Disguise mechanic wires to the R.A.M.I. operational register's case-reveal procedure, NOT through Detective subtype since this card is a Vampire Cleric per Scryfall canon), Orzhov institutional identity (the canonical Syndicate eclipsed-sun signet visible on chest armor and architecture per the `orzhov-signet` symbol node). This is the structural case the Agency's institutional-autonomy thesis predicts: a Vampire Cleric whose institutional secondary identity is an Orzhov-recruited operative serving Agency casework as a Disguise-mechanic-flavored auxiliary — extraction-faction labor reassigned to investigation-faction work without losing the medallion. The Vampire Cleric subtype (vs. Detective) parallels the Agency Coroner's Ogre Cleric subtype on the cleric-as-Agency-auxiliary pattern. Symbol-layer cross-edge: `symbols: ["orzhov-signet"]`; character-layer cross-edge: `characters: ["agency", "orzhov-syndicate"]`.
fixes.rationale: Correct line-82 type-line attribution. Sanguine Savior is canonically a Vampire Cleric per Scryfall, NOT a Detective. Rewire the dual-coded thesis from 'Detective subtype' to 'Disguise mechanic' since Disguise is what mechanically wires the card to the Agency operational register (the Detective subtype is canonically only on 4 of the 14 cohort cards). Add the auxiliary-function framing parallel to Agency Coroner.

fixes.before: - **The Agency is institutionally autonomous from but operationally entangled with the ten Ravnican guilds.** Cards in the corpus carry Agency operational register (detective, investigate, case, disguise) but may also carry guild-watermark coding (Azorius for the Burden-of-Proof lawyer-detective register; Simic for Mistway Spy's head-tendril species coding; Selesnya-Conclave-body for Crowd-Control Warden's centaur morphology; Orzhov for Sanguine Savior's chest-medallion eclipsed-sun signet). The frontmatter relation is `characters: ["agency"]` for Agency-operative cards; if the same card is also institutionally a guild operative (e.g., Sanguine Savior as Orzhov-recruited Detective), both characters edges apply. Wave-38 extension wired Sanguine Savior dual-coded as `characters: ["agency", "orzhov-syndicate"]` AND `symbols: ["orzhov-signet"]` per the eclipsed-sun jewelry-form documentation on the symbol node.
fixes.after: - **The Agency is institutionally autonomous from but operationally entangled with the ten Ravnican guilds.** Cards in the corpus carry Agency operational register (detective, investigate, case, disguise) but may also carry guild-watermark coding (Azorius for the Burden-of-Proof lawyer-detective register; Simic for Mistway Spy's head-tendril species coding; Selesnya-Conclave-body for Crowd-Control Warden's centaur morphology; Orzhov for Sanguine Savior's chest-medallion eclipsed-sun signet). The frontmatter relation is `characters: ["agency"]` for Agency-operative cards; if the same card is also institutionally a guild operative (e.g., Sanguine Savior as Orzhov-recruited Vampire Cleric serving the Agency via Disguise mechanic), both characters edges apply. Wave-38 extension wired Sanguine Savior dual-coded as `characters: ["agency", "orzhov-syndicate"]` AND `symbols: ["orzhov-signet"]` per the eclipsed-sun jewelry-form documentation on the symbol node.
fixes.rationale: Correct line-125 Caveats locus. Sanguine Savior is a Vampire Cleric (Scryfall canon), not a Detective.

fixes.before: - **Agency officers are unified by FUNCTION, not creature-type.** Pre-wave-38 corpus-internal pattern reading skewed toward Sphinx-coded Agency operatives (Agency Outfitter as Sphinx Detective being the most visible). Wave 38's extension undermines that pattern: the Agency now includes a Living Conundrum body-elemental built of bound case-files, a centaur Crowd-Control Warden, a Goblin Artificer Gadget Technician, a Faerie (corpus has Faerie Snoop coded criminal-side, but Furtive Courier as pointy-eared sprite-coded courier is Agency-side), a Vampire Cleric Sanguine Savior, and a Crocodile Elf hybrid. The unifying register is the Detective creature subtype + Disguise / Investigate / Case mechanic + flavor-attribution to the Agency or its Foundway Associates network — NOT creature type. Bundle copy should anchor on the institutional-function thesis (R.A.M.I. as the cross-species cross-guild detective bureau) rather than any creature-type-coded sub-roster.
fixes.after: - **Agency officers are unified by FUNCTION, not creature-type.** Pre-wave-38 corpus-internal pattern reading skewed toward Sphinx-coded Agency operatives (Agency Outfitter as Sphinx Detective being the most visible). Wave 38's extension undermines that pattern: the Agency now includes a Living Conundrum body-elemental built of bound case-files, a Centaur Soldier Crowd-Control Warden, a Goblin Artificer Gadget Technician, a Merfolk Advisor Furtive Courier (pointy-eared sprite-coded but canonically Merfolk per Scryfall), a Vampire Cleric Sanguine Savior, and an Elf Crocodile Detective. Per Scryfall canonical type-lines, only 4 of the 14 corpus-Agency cards carry the Detective creature subtype (Agency Outfitter = Sphinx Detective, Mistway Spy = Merfolk Detective, Reckless Detective = Devil Detective, Undercover Crocodelf = Elf Crocodile Detective). The unifying register across the full 14-card cohort is the **Disguise / Investigate / Case mechanic + flavor-attribution to the Agency or its Foundway Associates network + canonical MKM-set membership** — NOT the Detective creature subtype, which is canonical on only 4 of 14 cards. Bundle copy should anchor on the institutional-function thesis (R.A.M.I. as the cross-species cross-guild detective bureau) rather than any creature-type-coded sub-roster.
fixes.rationale: Correct line-127 Caveats body. The self-stated 'unified by function not creature-type' thesis is right; the supporting prose enumeration had the wrong type-line for Crowd-Control Warden (Centaur Soldier, not 'centaur Crowd-Control Warden' which was ambiguous), and stated the unifying register as 'Detective creature subtype + Disguise / Investigate / Case mechanic' when canonically the Detective subtype is on only 4 of 14 cards. The corrected version reframes the unifying register as Disguise / Investigate / Case + flavor + set-membership, dropping the Detective-subtype claim from the unifying-register description.

fixes.before: - **Sanguine Savior** (Murders at Karlov Manor no. 230) — chest-medallion form, dual-context. Valera Lutfullina art. The eclipsed-sun signet embossed onto the chest armor of a winged Detective Vampire Cleric carrying an unconscious companion through foggy gothic skies; the same sun-motif also visible on a distant spire in the background skyline. This is the canonical *Orzhov-recruited operative seconded to R.A.M.I.* dual-coded case — the figure is institutionally Orzhov (medallion-of-master form per the canonical duality, indicating chosen / status-marker rather than branded / debt-marker) AND operationally an Agency Detective. The card simultaneously anchors `characters: ["orzhov-syndicate", "agency"]` and `symbols: ["orzhov-signet"]`. The post-Phyrexian-Invasion cross-guild Agency staffing pattern documented on [[agency]] is visible here in a single character's iconography.
fixes.after: - **Sanguine Savior** (Murders at Karlov Manor no. 230) — chest-medallion form, dual-context. Valera Lutfullina art. The eclipsed-sun signet embossed onto the chest armor of a winged Vampire Cleric (per Scryfall canonical type-line: `Creature — Vampire Cleric`, NOT Detective despite MKM-cohort membership) carrying an unconscious companion through foggy gothic skies; the same sun-motif also visible on a distant spire in the background skyline. This is the canonical *Orzhov-recruited operative seconded to R.A.M.I.* dual-coded case — the figure is institutionally Orzhov (medallion-of-master form per the canonical duality, indicating chosen / status-marker rather than branded / debt-marker) AND operationally an Agency auxiliary (the Disguise mechanic on the card wires it to the R.A.M.I. case-reveal procedure without the Detective subtype, mirroring Agency Coroner's Ogre Cleric / non-Detective pattern). The card simultaneously anchors `characters: ["orzhov-syndicate", "agency"]` and `symbols: ["orzhov-signet"]`. The post-Phyrexian-Invasion cross-guild Agency staffing pattern documented on [[agency]] is visible here in a single character's iconography.
fixes.rationale: Correct line-52 Sanguine Savior description. Scryfall canon: Vampire Cleric, not Detective. Reroute the Agency-operational claim from 'Agency Detective' (false) to 'Agency auxiliary via Disguise mechanic' (true). Add the Agency Coroner parallel for type-line non-Detective Agency-cohort precedent.

fixes.before: - [[agency]] — Dual-coded Agency roster card. Agency operational function (Disguise + Detective subtype wire to the R.A.M.I. operational register) and Orzhov institutional identity (the canonical eclipsed-sun signet visible on the chest medallion and on the distant spire per the `orzhov-signet` symbol node) coexist on the card. This is the structural case the Agency's institutional-autonomy thesis predicts: a Detective whose institutional secondary identity is an Orzhov-recruited operative — extraction-faction labor reassigned to investigation-faction work without losing the medallion. Cross-edges: [[orzhov-syndicate]], [[orzhov-signet]]. `[MTG Wiki: Disguise (mechanic)]` `[MTG Wiki: Ravnican Agency of Magicological Investigations]` `[MTG Wiki: Orzhov Syndicate]`
fixes.after: - [[agency]] — Dual-coded Agency roster card. Agency operational function (Disguise mechanic wires to the R.A.M.I. operational register's case-reveal procedure, NOT through Detective subtype — per Scryfall canon, Sanguine Savior is `Creature — Vampire Cleric`, not a Detective) and Orzhov institutional identity (the canonical eclipsed-sun signet visible on the chest medallion and on the distant spire per the `orzhov-signet` symbol node) coexist on the card. This is the structural case the Agency's institutional-autonomy thesis predicts: a Vampire Cleric whose institutional secondary identity is an Orzhov-recruited operative serving Agency casework as a Disguise-mechanic-flavored auxiliary — extraction-faction labor reassigned to investigation-faction work without losing the medallion. Mirrors the Agency Coroner (Ogre Cleric, not Detective) pattern at the cleric-as-Agency-auxiliary structural precedent. Cross-edges: [[orzhov-syndicate]], [[orzhov-signet]]. `[Scryfall: mkm/230 type_line; MTG Wiki: Disguise (mechanic), Ravnican Agency of Magicological Investigations, Orzhov Syndicate]`
fixes.rationale: Correct line-107 card-body Connections bullet. Scryfall canon: Vampire Cleric (not Detective). Reroute the Agency-operational claim from 'Detective subtype' (false) to 'Disguise mechanic' (true).

verification_command: cd 'C:\Users\alexa\Desktop\Bulk Graph Bundler' && curl -s 'https://api.scryfall.com/cards/named?exact=Sanguine+Savior&set=mkm' -A 'Mozilla/5.0' | grep -o '"type_line":"[^"]*"' | head -1; curl -s 'https://api.scryfall.com/cards/named?exact=Furtive+Courier&set=mkm' -A 'Mozilla/5.0' | grep -o '"type_line":"[^"]*"' | head -1; curl -s 'https://api.scryfall.com/cards/named?exact=Gadget+Technician&set=mkm' -A 'Mozilla/5.0' | grep -o '"type_line":"[^"]*"' | head -1
uncertainty_notes: (1) Scryfall API was successfully queried during this dispatch for all 14 MKM agency.md cohort cards; the type-line attributions in this finding are direct API responses, not WebSearch snippets. The 3-card misattribution claim (Furtive Courier, Gadget Technician, Sanguine Savior) is high-confidence falsifiable per `bbl-verify-api-claims-by-calling-the-api`. (2) The fix preserves all the substantive thesis content of the original prose (Agency-operational-function, Orzhov-institutional-identity, dual-coded auxiliary, post-Phyrexian-Invasion staffing) while correcting the Detective-subtype claims to match Scryfall canon. The structural finding (cards are Agency-functional via Disguise / flavor / set-membership, not via Detective subtype) is consistent with the self-stated Caveats line-127 'unified by function not creature-type' thesis — the fixes make the body match what the Caveats already said. (3) The Reckless Detective 'goblinoid' → 'devil-figure' fix at agency.md line 72 is a separate minor visual-misidentification that the fix also corrects since it's adjacent context; if the operator prefers a strictly Detective-subtype-only fix, that bullet can be skipped (the Reckless Detective IS canonically a Devil Detective per Scryfall, so the Detective subtype attribution on this card is correct; only the 'goblinoid' visual descriptor is wrong). (4) The 5 non-creature agency.md cohort cards (Burden of Proof, Curious Inquiry, Eliminate the Impossible, Case of the Burning Masks, Expose the Culprit) are correctly described as non-creatures in the body — the misattribution is specific to the 3 named creature cards plus the type-line summary in the Caveats.
